items.title: Default to function-scope fixtures
items.prefer: Function scope unless the fixture is provably immutable AND expensive (model load, DB connection).
items.why: Cross-test pollution from shared mutable state is one of the most common flakiness causes.
items.example: Embedding model: `scope='session'` is fine. Mutable dict: `scope='function'`, always.

items.title: Put shared fixtures in `conftest.py`
items.prefer: Define common fixtures in `tests/conftest.py`; pytest auto-discovers them in every test below.
items.why: One definition; consistent setup; easy to change globally.
items.example: Nested `conftest.py` files inherit downward — useful for area-specific fixtures.

items.title: Use built-in fixtures before writing your own
items.why: Battle-tested; well-documented; auto-cleanup; less code to maintain.

items.title: Patch where the dependency is *used*
items.avoid: Patching the library's own module path.
items.prefer: Patch the binding inside the module under test (`ragapp.summarizer.OpenAI`).
items.why: Imports create local bindings; patching the original doesn't affect them.

items.title: Prefer dependency injection over patching
items.avoid: Stacks of `@patch` decorators on every test.
items.prefer: Have the unit accept its collaborators as parameters; tests pass fakes.
items.why: DI makes both production and test code cleaner; patching is a workaround for code that isn't DI-friendly.
items.example:
```python
# Instead of patching the module-level client:
class Summarizer:
    def __init__(self, client): self.client = client
# Tests just pass a fake client; no patch needed.
```
